title: Apple ने बढ़ाए MacBook और iPad के दाम, जानिए कितनी महंगी हुईं डिवाइस और क्या है वजह
description:
Apple hikes prices MacBook and iPad: Apple ने आखिरकार अपने MacBook और iPad की कीमतों में बढ़ोतरी कर दी है। पिछले कई दिनों से इस बात की चर्चा चल रही थी कि कंपनी अपने प्रोडक्ट्स महंगे करने वाली है और अब इसकी आधिकारिक पुष्टि हो गई है। कंपनी का कहना है कि मेमोरी (RAM) और.
The post Apple ने बढ़ाए MacBook और iPad के दाम, जानिए कितनी महंगी हुईं डिवाइस और क्या है वजह appeared first on Dainik Savera Times.
content:
Apple hikes prices MacBook and iPad: Apple ने आखिरकार अपने MacBook और iPad की कीमतों में बढ़ोतरी कर दी है। पिछले कई दिनों से इस बात की चर्चा चल रही थी कि कंपनी अपने प्रोडक्ट्स महंगे करने वाली है और अब इसकी आधिकारिक पुष्टि हो गई है। कंपनी का कहना है कि मेमोरी (RAM) और स्टोरेज चिप्स की बढ़ती कीमतों के कारण उसे यह फैसला लेना पड़ा है। भारत में भी Apple की आधिकारिक वेबसाइट पर नई कीमतें लागू कर दी गई हैं।
MacBook और iPad कितने महंगे हुए?
MacBook Neo: Apple की भारतीय वेबसाइट पर फिलहाल MacBook Neo का केवल एक मॉडल 79,900 रुपये में सूचीबद्ध है। यदि यह 512GB स्टोरेज वाला मॉडल है, तो इसकी कीमत में कोई बदलाव नहीं किया गया है।
MacBook Air: 13 इंच MacBook Air की कीमत अब 1,49,900 रुपये हो गई है। लॉन्च के समय इसकी शुरुआती कीमत 1,19,900 रुपये थी। वहीं 15 इंच MacBook Air, जिसे पहले 1,44,900 रुपये में लॉन्च किया गया था, अब 1,79,900 रुपये में मिलेगा। यानी पहले जिस कीमत में 15 इंच मॉडल खरीदा जा सकता था, अब लगभग उसी कीमत में 13 इंच मॉडल मिलेगा।
iPad Air: 11 इंच iPad Air की नई कीमत 89,900 रुपये कर दी गई है। 13 इंच iPad Air अब 1,19,900 रुपये में उपलब्ध है। दोनों मॉडल अब अपनी लॉन्च कीमतों से अधिक महंगे हो चुके हैं। मीडिया रिपोर्ट्स के मुताबिक, कीमतों में बदलाव से पहले गुरुवार सुबह Apple का ऑनलाइन स्टोर कुछ समय के लिए बंद भी रहा था। बाद में वेबसाइट पर नई कीमतें दिखाई देने लगीं।
Apple ने क्या बताया?
कंपनी के अनुसार, पूरी कंज्यूमर इलेक्ट्रॉनिक्स इंडस्ट्री इस समय बड़ी चुनौती का सामना कर रही है। AI (आर्टिफिशियल इंटेलिजेंस) डेटा सेंटरों के तेजी से बढ़ने के कारण मेमोरी और स्टोरेज चिप्स की मांग काफी बढ़ गई है। इससे इन कंपोनेंट्स की लागत में तेज उछाल आया है। Apple का कहना है कि अब कंपनी के लिए बढ़ी हुई लागत को खुद वहन करना मुश्किल हो गया है। इसी वजह से कुछ प्रोडक्ट्स की कीमतें बढ़ानी पड़ रही हैं। हालांकि कंपनी ने यह भी कहा कि वह लागत कम करने के लिए लगातार नए समाधान तलाश रही है।
The post Apple ने बढ़ाए MacBook और iPad के दाम, जानिए कितनी महंगी हुईं डिवाइस और क्या है वजह appeared first on Dainik Savera Times.

title: दुल्हन के दो दावेदार, सात फेरों के 24 घंटे बाद एंट्री, फिर हाई-वोल्टेज ड्रामा
description:
Two contenders for the bride : बैकुंठपुर। शादी के मंडप में लिए गए सात फेरे अभी 24 घंटे भी पूरे नहीं हुए थे कि दुल्हन के नए घर में ऐसा ड्रामा शुरू हो गया, जिसने पूरे गांव को हैरान कर दिया। सुहागरात की रस्में भी पूरी नहीं हुई थीं कि अचानक दुल्हन का प्रेमी पहुंच.
The post दुल्हन के दो दावेदार, सात फेरों के 24 घंटे बाद एंट्री, फिर हाई-वोल्टेज ड्रामा appeared first on Dainik Savera Times.
content:
Two contenders for the bride : बैकुंठपुर। शादी के मंडप में लिए गए सात फेरे अभी 24 घंटे भी पूरे नहीं हुए थे कि दुल्हन के नए घर में ऐसा ड्रामा शुरू हो गया, जिसने पूरे गांव को हैरान कर दिया। सुहागरात की रस्में भी पूरी नहीं हुई थीं कि अचानक दुल्हन का प्रेमी पहुंच गया और उसे अपने साथ ले जाने की जिद पर अड़ गया। इसके बाद जो हुआ, वह देर रात तक थाने में चलता रहा और आखिरकार मामला प्रेम, परिवार और रिश्तों के टकराव की मिसाल बन गया।
रात 2 बजे तक समझाती रही पुलिस
दूल्हा-दुल्हन, उनके परिजन और प्रेमी को पुलिस रात 2 बजे तक समझाती रही, लेकिन कोई फायदा नहीं हुआ। दूल्हे पक्ष ने रिसेप्शन रद्द कर दी और दुल्हन पक्ष पर धोखाधड़ी का आरोप लगाते हुए तलाक की मांग कर दी। इस दौरान पुलिस ने प्रेमी को लॉकअप में बंद कर दिया और दुल्हन को लौटना पड़ा। मनेन्द्रगढ़ इलाके में एक विवाह ने उस वक्त विवाद का रूप धारण कर लिया, जब शादी के महज दूसरे दिन ही दुल्हन का कथित प्रेमी उसके ससुराल पहुंच गया और दावा किया कि वह युवती (दुल्हन) के साथ 3 साल से रिलेशनशिप में है।
आज भी चर्चित है ये मामला
ये बात कुछ दिन पहले की है, लेकिन आज भी चर्चित है। दोनों एक-दूसरे से प्यार करते हैं। इसलिए अपनी गर्लफ्रेंड को किसी और की होते नहीं देख सकता। बताया जा रहा है कि युवती की रीति-रिवाज और धूमधाम के साथ शादी हुई थी। परिवार शादी के बाद रिसेप्शन की तैयारी में जुटा था। इसी बीच प्रेमी अचानक दुल्हन के ससुराल पहुंच गया। यहां उसने दुल्हन से मिलने की जिद शुरू कर दी। देखते ही देखते मामला इतना बढ़ गया कि दोनों परिवार आमने-सामने आ गए और घर के बाहर भीड़ लग गई।
3 साल से संपर्क में थे दोनों
प्रत्यक्षदर्शियों के मुताबिक, प्रेमी लगातार यह कहता रहा कि युवती पिछले 3 साल से उसके साथ रिश्ते में थी और परिवार के दबाव में शादी हुई। ससुराल पक्ष इस घटनाक्रम से हैरान और नाराज नजर आया। शादी के दो दिन बाद अचानक यह विवाद चर्चा का विषय बन गया। दुल्हन के मायके वालों को भी थाने बुलाया गया। उसके बाद लडक़ी पक्ष ने मनेन्द्रगढ़ सिटी कोतवाली पहुंच जानकारी दी। पुलिस ने मौके पर पहुंच कर हालात को संभालने की कोशिश की। विवाद बढ़ता देख पुलिस दूल्हा-दुल्हन और कथित प्रेमी को थाने लेकर पहुंची। लडक़ी के मायके और दुल्हे पक्ष के लोग भी पहुंच गए। यहां रात करीब 2 बजे तक पूछताछ चलती रही, लेकिन कई घंटे तक चले ड्रामे के बाद कोई समाधान नहीं निकला। थाना प्रभारी ने दुल्हन के परिजन व दूल्हे को बुलाकर आपस में बात भी करवाई, लेकिन नतीजा कुछ नहीं निकल पाया। दूल्हे ने थाना में साफ कहा कि दुल्हन को अब वह नहीं रखेगा। फिर अपने परिवार के साथ निकल गया।
देररात तक जिद पर अड़ा रहा प्रेमी
बताया जा रहा है कि प्रेमी थाने में भी जिद पर अड़ा रहा और युवती के साथ रहने की बात कहता रहा। मामले में पुलिस ने शांति भंग होने की आशंका के चलते लॉकअप में बंद कर दिया। घटनाक्रम के बाद दूल्हा और उसका परिवार बेहद नाराज हो गया। शादी के बाद होने वाला रिसेप्शन भी रद्द कर दिया गया। इससे दुल्हन को बिना रिसेप्शन थाने से ही अपने मायके लौटना पड़ गया। शादी के लिए इलाके में तरह-तरह की चर्चाएं शुरू हो गई है। फिलहाल पुलिस मामले की जांच कर रही है और दोनों पक्षों से पूछताछ जारी है। लकड़ी पक्ष ने थाने में कोई शिकायत दर्ज नहीं कराई है, जबकि दूल्हे ने शिकायत कर कहा कि मुझे अब तलाक चाहिए, क्योंकि धोखे में रख कर मेरी शादी कराई गई है। मेरे पूरा सामान लौटाया जाए।

title: জামুগুৰিহাটত উত্তৰ জামুগুৰি কেন্দ্ৰীয় অখণ্ড হৰি কীৰ্ত্তন সমিতিৰ ৬৮তম প্ৰতিষ্ঠা দিৱস পালন
description:
ডিজিটেল সংবাদ, জামুগুৰিহাটঃ জামুগুৰিহাটৰ বালিজুৰিৰ উত্তৰ জামুগুৰি কেন্দ্ৰীয় অখণ্ড হৰি কীৰ্ত্তন সমিতিৰ আজি ৬৮তম প্ৰতিষ্ঠা দিৱস। এই প্ৰতিষ্ঠা দিৱস উপলক্ষে নৱ নিৰ্মিত কাৰ্যালয়ৰ শুভ উদ্বোধনী অনুষ্ঠান অনুষ্ঠিত হয়।
কাৰ্যসূচী অনুসৰি পুৱাই ধৰ্ম ধ্বজা উত্তোলন কৰে কেন্দ্ৰীয় অখণ্ড হৰি কীৰ্ত্তন সমিতিৰ সভাপতি তুলসী ভট্ট ৰায়ে। অনুষ্ঠানটিত কেন্দ্ৰীয় অখণ্ড হৰি কীৰ্ত্তন সমিতিখনৰ প্ৰতিষ্ঠাপক সকলৰ প্ৰতিচ্ছবিত পূষ্পাঞ্জলী অৰ্পণেৰে শ্ৰদ্ধা নিবেদন কৰা হয়।

ইফালে, উত্তৰ জামুগুৰি কেন্দ্ৰীয় অখণ্ড হৰি কীৰ্ত্তন সমিতিৰ নৱ নিৰ্মিত মুখ্য কাৰ্যালয়টি শুভ উদ্বোধন কৰে উত্তৰ নাগশংকৰ গাঁও পঞ্চায়তৰ সভানেত্ৰী দুলুমণি দত্ত শইকীয়াই। ইয়াৰ পিছতে অনুষ্ঠিত হোৱা মুকলি সভাৰ পিছতে সন্ধিয়াৰ ভাগত ভজন কীৰ্ত্তনৰ জৰিয়তে অনুষ্ঠানটিৰ সামৰণি পৰিব বুলি জানিবলৈ দিয়ে কেন্দ্ৰীয় অখণ্ড হৰি কীৰ্ত্তন সমিতিৰ সম্পাদক কিশোৰ পৌড়েলে ।

description:

title: US-Iran agree to setup direct channel with IRGC to de-escalate conflict, says VP Vance
description:
Washington DC [US], June 26 : US Vice President JD Vance confirmed that the US and Iran have agreed to set up a direct deconfliction channel with military representatives from both countries, reported Al Jazeera.
The arrangement involves representatives from Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) and the US Army's Central Command (CENTCOM) being stationed in Doha, Qatar.
US Vice President JD Vance, in an interview with a British news site, said that Tehran and Washington have agreed to set up a direct channel with Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), in order to reduce the risk of further conflict, Al Jazeera reported.
Speaking to the British news site UnHerd, JD Vance said the arrangement would involve Iranian and US military officials meeting in Doha to work through disputes, according to the report by Al Jazeera on Thursday.
"They were like, 'OK, fine, we'll send somebody from the IRGC to go hang out in Doha with somebody from [U.S. Central Command]' and that's how we're going to settle a lot of these disputes", Vance described discussions with Iranian officials as per Al Jazeera.
 It was also noted that Vance mentioned the UAE also have conversations with Iran and the IRGC on the types of "economic incentives".
He said the UAE is "having conversations with the Iranians that have never happened before, including with the IRGC, about various types of economic incentives".
His remarks come as the United States and Iran continue to engage following the Lake Lucerne technical talks.
 Meanwhile, the United States and the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) on Thursday (local time) reaffirmed their strategic partnership at a ministerial meeting in Bahrain's Manama, underscoring their commitment to regional security, diplomacy with Iran, stability in Syria and Lebanon, and reconstruction efforts in Gaza.
 The meeting was co-chaired by US Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Bahraini Foreign Minister Abdullatif bin Rashid Al Zayani, the current chairman of the GCC Ministerial Council, and attended by foreign ministers from GCC member states and GCC Secretary General Jasem Albudaiwi.
According to the joint statement, Secretary Rubio "reinforced the enduring US commitment to GCC security," while the ministers reaffirmed their "strong commitment to the US-GCC strategic partnership."
The ministers welcomed the June 17 memorandum of understanding (MOU) between the United States and Iran and acknowledged the mediation efforts of Pakistan and Qatar. They emphasised "the need to maintain momentum and unity as negotiations proceed toward a more permanent end to hostilities" and reiterated their shared objective of "preventing Iran from ever developing or otherwise acquiring a nuclear weapon.
"The statement said lasting regional peace requires addressing "the full spectrum of Iran's threats, including its ballistic missiles, drones, and support of proxies in the region.
"The ministers also stressed the importance of reopening the Strait of Hormuz, saying that "free, unconditional, and unrestricted navigation, including the right of transit passage as guaranteed under international law, remains essential to regional and global security." They rejected "any tolls, fees, or attempts to assert control over the Strait" and welcomed Oman and the International Maritime Organization's (IMO)evacuation plan for more than 11,000 stranded seafarers.
The joint statement noted that "any trade and investment with Iran is conditional and reversible," depending on Tehran's compliance with the MOU and any final agreement, an end to destabilising activities, and the creation of conditions necessary for economic engagement. (ANI)

title: ll about Sarah Jessica Parker’s rise to success
description: Sarah Jessica Parker's rise from financial insecurity to a net worth of USD 200 million is a testament to her resilience. Raised with eight siblings in a challenging environment, she transformed struggles into ambition. Parker often took on roles she found unfulfilling just to pay the bills, yet her unwavering focus on her dreams propelled her from a child actor into a celebrated global icon, embodying the spirit of perseverance.

title: চৰাইদেউ প্ৰেছ গীল্ডৰ উদ্যোগত সোণাৰিৰ বিধায়ক ধৰ্মেশ্বৰ কোঁৱৰক সম্বৰ্ধনা জ্ঞাপন
description:
ডিজিটেল সংবাদ, চৰাইদেউঃ চৰাইদেউ জিলাৰ সোণাৰি বিধানসভা সমষ্টিৰ পৰা দ্বিতীয়বাৰৰ বাবে বিধায়ক হিচাপে নিৰ্বাচিত হোৱা জনপ্ৰিয় তথা দায়িত্বশীল বিধায়ক ধৰ্মেশ্বৰ কোঁৱৰক শুকুৰবাৰে চৰাইদেউ প্ৰেছ গীল্ডৰ তৰফৰ পৰা সম্বৰ্ধনা জনোৱা হয়।
চৰাইদেউ প্ৰেছ গীল্ড কাৰ্যালয়ত আয়োজিত অনুষ্ঠানত বিধায়ক ধৰ্মেশ্বৰ কোঁৱৰে সম্বৰ্ধনা গ্ৰহণ কৰি প্ৰেছ গীল্ডৰ বিষয়ববীয়া আৰু সদস্যসকলৰ প্ৰতি আন্তৰিক কৃতজ্ঞতা জ্ঞাপন কৰে। তেওঁ কয় যে- গণতন্ত্ৰৰ এক শক্তিশালী স্তম্ভ হিচাপে সংবাদ মাধ্যমৰ ভূমিকা অতি গুৰুত্বপূৰ্ণ। সমাজৰ স্বাৰ্থত চৰাইদেউ জিলাৰ সাংবাদিকসকলে নিষ্ঠা, সততা আৰু দায়িত্ববোধেৰে কৰি অহা কাম-কাজ সঁচাকৈয়ে প্ৰশংসনীয় বুলি মন্তব্য কৰে তেওঁ৷
উক্ত অনুষ্ঠানত চৰাইদেউ প্ৰেছ গীল্ডৰ সভাপতি সূৰ্য্য বড়া, উপদেষ্টা সঞ্জীৱ গগৈ, সাধাৰণ সম্পাদক অৰূপ দিহিঙীয়া, জ্যেষ্ঠ সাংবাদিক হৰি ফুকন, ভাস্কৰ ভাজনি, প্ৰাক্তন সাধাৰণ সম্পাদক মিটুপৰ্ণ গগৈ, কেশৱ গগৈকে ধৰি প্ৰেছ গীল্ডৰ বিষয়ববীয়া, সদস্য আৰু জিলাখনৰ বহু সংখ্যক সাংবাদিক উপস্থিত থাকে।

title: CFOs are bullish on their own companies—even as they turn bearish on the economy
description: Even as macro concerns deepen, CFOs are leaning into risk, reshaping talent, and betting on AI to drive company-specific gains.
content:
Good morning. CFOs can’t control the economy, but they increasingly believe they can control the outcome for their own companies.



That tension—what Deloitte calls a “paradox of promise versus pessimism”—is a defining theme in the firm’s North American Q2 2026 CFO Signals survey, Ed Hardy, U.S. financial services leader at Deloitte, told me.



About a third (33%) of respondents said the economy in North America is bad, compared to only 5% who felt this way in the first quarter. Meanwhile, 90% said they’re significantly or somewhat more optimistic about the future financial prospects of their company. The findings are based on a survey of 200 CFOs from across the U.S., Canada, and Mexico, at companies with at least $1 billion in revenue.



“The macroeconomic outlook has dipped for two consecutive quarters,” Hardy said. “But at the same time, CFOs’ confidence in their own ability to execute strategy and navigate challenges is as strong as it’s been in several quarters.”



The divergence reflects a deeper shift in how finance chiefs are operating. “They’ve built the muscle,” Hardy said.



That is translating into action. Despite concerns about market valuations, 59% of CFOs think now is a good time to take calculated risks (up from 48% last quarter), including accessing debt markets and raising capital. Many believe their companies offer a “unique value signature,” Hardy noted, even in a choppy environment.


Courtesy of Deloitte



Still, external risks remain front and center. Inflation and broader economic conditions continue to rank among the top concerns, with roughly half of CFOs citing inflation as a key issue to monitor in the months ahead. While the Federal Reserve’s steady rate posture may be supporting some risk-taking, it has not eliminated underlying unease.



At the same time, the role of technology—particularly AI—is more complex than a straightforward confidence booster. Companies are moving quickly to deploy and scale AI, but questions around return on investment, cost management, and governance persist, Hardy said. Finance leaders are also grappling with how to measure value beyond traditional cost savings, as AI opens the door to new capabilities rather than just efficiencies.


Courtesy of Deloitte



Perhaps the most pressing challenge tied to that shift is talent. In the survey, talent ranked as the top risk factor, cited by 51% of CFOs—higher than any other internal or external concern. The issue spans hiring, retention, and, increasingly, upskilling.



Finance functions are being forced to rethink their workforce mix. Alongside traditional accounting expertise, companies are beginning to integrate new roles such as prompt engineers and AI specialists. But the need for experienced finance professionals remains critical, particularly as “humans in the loop” are responsible for validating outputs and managing risk.



“It’s not just about adding new skills,” Hardy said. “It’s about rethinking how you attract, retain, and incentivize a very different mix of talent.”



Taken together, the findings suggest CFOs are entering a new phase—one defined less by reactive crisis management and more by calibrated confidence.



Have a good weekend.Sheryl EstradaSheryl.Estrada@fortune.com
This story was originally featured on Fortune.com

title: Wet Leg team up with HorsegiirL for high-octane remix of ‘CPR’
description:
It will feature on a deluxe version of their 2025 album 'Moisturizer'
The post Wet Leg team up with HorsegiirL for high-octane remix of ‘CPR’ appeared first on NME.
content:
Wet Leg have shared a new remix of their single ‘CPR’ by HorsegiirL – listen to it below.

READ MORE: HorsegiirL – ‘Nature Is Healing’ review: divine equine dance bangers that balance humour with heart

The high-octane heavy beats version of the track is significantly different from the original, with HorsegiirL adding a verse of her own. She also performed the track with the band at Coachella 2026 earlier this year.
The remix is set to feature on an expanded version of the band’s 2025 LP ‘Moisturizer’, which will feature three previously unreleased live songs along with their previously released remix of ‘mangetout’ by The Dare and Fontaines D.C. DJs’ version of hit single ‘Catch These Fists’.
The original album landed at Number 22 in NME’s 50 best albums of 2025 list, and reached Number One in the UK albums chart.


Reviewing the album, NME awarded it four stars praising the record as seeing them “return smarter, sexier and altogether stronger” than ever.
Rhian Teasdale and co. caught up with NME at the 2026 BRIT Awards, where they were nominated for both British Group and Alternative/Rock categories.
Their second LP went on to be nominated for three awards at the Grammys and, since then, the band have taken part in the huge, star-studded ‘Help(2)’ album for War Child.
“War Child is such an important charity because the world is just scary,” the singer told us. “It’s a really scary place right now. So, being in the position that we’re in, it was literally the least anyone could do. It was easy for us to do something like that.”
They are due to perform a series of outdoor UK dates including a show at Castlefield Bowl in Manchester on July 8, the Millennium Square in Leeds the day after (July 9) and London’s Alexandra Palace Park on July 10. You can purchase any remaining tickets here.
As for HorsegiirL she recently released her debut album ‘Nature Is Healing’ which we awarded four stars and said it was filled with “divine equine dance bangers that balance humour with heart”.
It added: “There’s plenty of speculation about whether HorsegiirL is a major-label gimmick, but the DJ has openly spoken about her queer underground rave origins. That experience has undeniably informed HorsegiirL’s confidence barnstorming through a wide diversity of dance genres without getting lost. She might be known for horsing around, but ‘Nature Is Healing’ proves you shouldn’t take Stella Stallion for a foal.”
The post Wet Leg team up with HorsegiirL for high-octane remix of ‘CPR’ appeared first on NME.

title: Goldman Sachs lifts India's CY26 GDP growth forecast after US-Iran deal; cuts CAD estimates - Details
description: Goldman Sachs India GDP: In its latest report titled India: Improved macro outlook after the US-Iran deal, the global investment bank said it has revised its forecasts after the sharp decline in crude oil prices reduced risks to the Indian economy.
content: Goldman Sachs India GDP: Global brokerage firm Goldman Sachs has lifted its outlook for India’s economic growth for calendar year 2026 after the recent US-Iran peace deal, besides lowering its inflation and current account deficit projections, citing easing oil prices and improving domestic economic conditions.Goldman Sachs lifts India GDP forecastIn its latest report titled India: Improved macro outlook after the US-Iran deal, the global investment bank said it has revised its forecasts after the sharp decline in crude oil prices reduced risks to the Indian economy."On balance, with the recent downward revision in the oil price forecast by our commodities team ($82/bbl average in Q3-Q4 CY26, vs. $92/bbl earlier and $75/bbl average in CY27, vs. $80/bbl earlier), we raise our real GDP growth forecast for CY26 by 0.3pp to 6.8% yoy, lower our headline inflation forecast by 0.2pp to 4.4% yoy and lower our current account deficit forecast by 0.2pp to 1.1% of GDP," the report said.‘India's economy remained resilient despite Middle East triggered disruptions’According to Goldman Sachs, India's economy has remained resilient despite the disruptions caused by the Middle East conflict, as government fiscal and quasi-fiscal measures helped cushion the impact of higher energy prices on consumers."The Indian economy remained resilient through the Middle-East shock, as fiscal and quasi-fiscal measures absorbed much of the increase in energy costs and limited pass-through to consumers," the report noted.The brokerage said stronger-than-expected economic activity in the first quarter of CY26, along with lower crude oil prices, prompted it to revise its growth outlook upward. India's real GDP growth in the first quarter came in at 7.8 per cent year-on-year, supported by resilient investment and robust services activity.While Goldman Sachs expects consumption growth to moderate during the second and third quarters due to the earlier increase in fuel prices, it believes the decline in oil prices has significantly reduced the need for further retail fuel price hikes, limiting additional pressure on household spending beyond the third quarter.Softer global commodity prices likely to reduce fiscal pressuresThe report added that softer global commodity prices are expected to reduce fiscal pressures. "The sharp correction in global urea prices should reduce upside risk to the fertilizer subsidy bill versus our earlier expectations... together with lower oil prices, should help ease near-term fiscal pressures," it said.On inflation, Goldman Sachs said lower crude oil prices have substantially reduced the risk of further increases in petrol and diesel prices and eased pressure on petrochemical products, leading to lower projections for both core and headline inflation.The report added that lower oil prices and stronger remittance inflows have improved India's external sector outlook."Overall, we lower our current account deficit forecast for CY26 further by 0.2pp to 1.1% of GDP," Goldman Sachs said, adding that it now expects a balance of payments surplus of 0.7 per cent of GDP for the year.The brokerage, however, maintained that weather-related uncertainties and the impact of earlier fuel price increases could remain short-term headwinds for consumption before the economy gathers further momentum later in the year.(With ANI inputs)

title: এই গাঁৱৰ বাসিন্দাই মুখেৰে ক’ব নোৱাৰে কথা, শুনিবও নোৱাৰে একো শব্দ
description:
ডিজিটেল ডেস্ক: ভাৰত এখন বৈচিত্ৰ্যময় দেশ। এই দেশৰ প্ৰতিখন ৰাজ্য, প্ৰতিখন গাঁও নিজৰ স্বকীয় সংস্কৃতি, প্ৰাকৃতিক সৌন্দৰ্য আৰু জীৱনশৈলীৰ বাবে বিশেষভাৱে পৰিচিত। কিন্তু এইখন ভৰততে এনে এখন গাঁও আছে, যিখন গাঁৱত বিৰাজ কৰে কেৱল নীৰৱতা। এই বিশেষ গাঁওখনৰ নাম ডডকাই। ডডকাই গাঁও জম্মু-কাশ্মীৰৰ ডোডা জিলাৰ গণ্ডোহ তহচিলৰ ভালেছা অঞ্চলত অৱস্থিত। এই বিশেষ গাঁওখন শেহতীয়াভাৱে ‘Silent Village’ অৰ্থাৎ ‘নীৰৱ গাঁও’ বুলি পৰিচিত হৈছে।
পাহাৰেৰে আৱৰি থকা এই গাঁওখন প্ৰাকৃতিক সৌন্দৰ্যৰে ভৰপূৰ। সেউজীয়া অৰণ্য, শীতল বতাহ আৰু মনোমোহা পৰিৱেশে গাঁওখনক স্বৰ্গসম কৰি তোলে। কিন্তু এই মনোমোহা দৃশ্যৰ আঁৰত লুকাই আছে এক গভীৰ বেদনাদায়ক বাস্তৱতা। গাঁওখনৰ বহুতো বাসিন্দাই জন্মগতভাৱে শুনিব বা কথা ক'ব নোৱাৰে। বিশেষকৈ শিশুসকলৰ মাজত এই সমস্যা অধিক পৰিলক্ষিত হয়। প্ৰায় ১০৫টা পৰিয়ালে বাস কৰা এই গাঁওখনত ৯০ শতাংশ লোকে জন্মগত বধিৰতা আৰু বাকশক্তিহীনতাৰ সমস্যাত ভুগি আছে।
ইংগিতৰ ভাষাই যোগাযোগৰ একমাত্ৰ মাধ্যম
ইয়াত সাধাৰণ গাঁওৰ দৰে শিশুৰ হাঁহি, খেল-ধেমালি বা মানুহৰ কথোপকথনৰ শুনিবলৈ পোৱা নাযায়। ইয়াৰ পৰিৱৰ্তে গাঁওবাসীয়ে ইজনে সিজনৰ লগত ইংগিতৰ ভাষা (Sign Language) ব্যৱহাৰ কৰি যোগাযোগ কৰে।
বিজ্ঞানীসকলে কি কয়?
গাঁওৰ বয়োজ্যেষ্ঠসকলৰ মতে, ১৯০১ চনত প্ৰথমবাৰৰ বাবে এই সমস্যাৰ তথ্য লিপিবদ্ধ হৈছিল। তাৰ পিছৰে পৰা প্ৰজন্মৰ পিছত প্ৰজন্ম এই সমস্যা চলি আহিছে। বিজ্ঞানীসকলৰ মতে, ইয়াৰ মূল কাৰণ সম্ভৱতঃ বংশগত (Genetic)। একে সম্প্ৰদায় বা নিকট আত্মীয়ৰ মাজত বিবাহ অধিক হোৱাৰ ফলত এই জিনগত সমস্যা বংশানুক্ৰমে বিস্তাৰ লাভ কৰিছে। যদিও কিছু গাঁওবাসীয়ে ইয়াক এক অভিশাপ বুলি বিশ্বাস কৰে, তথাপি বৈজ্ঞানিকভাৱে জিনগত কাৰণকেই অধিক গ্ৰহণযোগ্য বুলি ধৰা হয়।
সামাজিক জীৱনত ইয়াৰ প্ৰভাৱ
এই সমস্যাই গাঁওবাসীক বহু সামাজিক অসুবিধাৰ সন্মুখীন কৰিছে। বহু পৰিয়ালে সন্তানৰ ভৱিষ্যৎক লৈ চিন্তিত হৈ থাকে। এনে পৰিয়ালৰ সৈতে বৈবাহিক সম্পৰ্ক স্থাপন কৰিবলৈও বহুতে অনীহা প্ৰকাশ কৰে। ফলত কিছুমান পৰিয়ালে এই গাঁও এৰি অন্য ঠাইত বসবাস কৰিবলৈ বাধ্য হৈছে।
ভাৰতীয় সেনাৰ সহায়ে জগাইছে নতুন আশা
সাম্প্ৰতিক সময়ত ভাৰতীয় সেনাৰ ৰাষ্ট্ৰীয় ৰাইফলছে এই গাঁওখনলৈ বিভিন্ন ধৰণৰ সহায় আগবঢ়াইছে। বিশেষভাৱে সক্ষম শিশুসকলৰ বাবে শিক্ষাৰ ব্যৱস্থা, ছাইন লেংগুৱেজ শিকোৱাৰ বাবে বিশেষজ্ঞ নিয়োগ, স্বাস্থ্যসেৱা, খাদ্য আৰু বস্ত্ৰৰ সুবিধা প্ৰদানৰ দৰে বহু পদক্ষেপ গ্ৰহণ কৰা হৈছে। এই প্ৰচেষ্টাসমূহে গাঁওখনৰ শিশুসকলৰ জীৱনত নতুন আশাৰ সঞ্চাৰ কৰিছে।

title: গহপুৰৰ মেধাৱী ছাত্ৰ গৌতম ফুকনৰ কৃতিত্ব...
From small parts to centre stage, the rise of Chhaya Kadam
description: Chhaya Kadam finds it hard to erase 2001 from her memory. It was a year when she lost both her brother and father. “I felt as though my world had collapsed. I was confined to my home. I knew I should step out and carry on with life, but I was unable to get myself to do that,” says the actor who also underwent two knee surgeries after damaging a ligament the same year.Kadam, who had acted in a few plays in school, was not interested in taking to the stage in college. She had taken to kabaddi until her damaged knees meant she had to stop pursuing the sport. The tragedy in her family during the same period left her disillusioned and morose.It was around this time that she saw a newspaper advertisement about an acting workshop by theatre veteran Waman Kendre in Mumbai. Kadam believed it was a good opportunity to meet new people instead of spending time with relatives or friends, who, in all probability, would discuss the turmoil in her life. The course turned out to be a blessing for her.“I enjoyed the process… I got to learn a lot, and not just acting. It taught me to be independent and shaped me as a human. It made me understand that one does not become an actor merely by attending workshops or doing photo shoots. It erased all such assumptions. I realised I had to get better at many things—for instance, I didn’t read the newspapers, and I had language and diction issues because I hailed from Konkan. The workshop developed me in more ways than I could have imagined,” says Kadam, who has starred in acclaimed Marathi films like Sairat and Fandry, as well as recent noteworthy ones like the Hindi comedy-drama Laapataa Ladies, Sister Midnight and the Grand Prix (Cannes)-winning Malayalam film All We Imagine as Light.W-Power List 2026 | Meet India's Top Self-Made Women of 2026Kendre, who saw her from close quarters, describes her as a hardworking and passionate artiste. “It was evident that Chhaya would stand out. She had a lot of potential and was serious about her work. She is an honest actor,” the former director of the National School of Drama tells Forbes India. He adds that she trained for two-and-a-half months for his directorial venture and her first play, Zulwa, and ended up doing around 250 shows. That laid the foundation for her as an actor.As Kadam fell in love with acting during the workshop, she decided to pursue it as a profession. It was not easy, though, to make inroads for someone who did not come from a film family. Kadam’s father was a mill worker from Kalina, Mumbai, and her mother was a housewife. She was the youngest among five siblings—two brothers and three sisters.When the acting breaks did not come through, she took up smaller assignments such as working as an assistant for a Hindi serial. The hunt for proper parts continued. In fact, when she got to know that Doordarshan was making a short film, she approached the makers for a role. The director told her that casting was done and that they needed an assistant instead. She agreed, thinking it would help her make connections. The film was being shot in Baramati, Maharashtra, and as luck would have it, the role Kadam was vying for fell into her lap. The lady who was supposed to play the part did not turn up the day before her scene was scheduled to be filmed. The team asked Kadam to fill in, and she faced the camera for the first time—for Phatacha Pani (Marathi).“I struggled a lot in those five years after the workshop… in fact, I got my first play [Zulwa] in 2006. My family did not support me… they knew nothing about this industry or my battles. But all the obstacles that came my way during that phase made me stronger,” says Kadam. “I did everything with utmost sincerity and dedication. Even if I got one scene in a film, I accepted that and did justice to the part. But the industry is such that when you get work, it does not mean you have arrived. You have to keep looking for newer opportunities. And it is difficult to get those… it can take many years.”It indeed took a while for Kadam to stamp her authority with her performances. Today, the Madgaon Express actor is known for bringing authenticity to the characters she essays and for her nuanced approach to her craft. “With acting, you literally give birth to a person—their walk, mannerisms, body language, backstory, even if it’s a fictional character,” she explains. “I am an observant person. I study people. After I read what has been offered to me, I work on the small details and decorate the character.”Also Read: W-Power 2026: Why Huma Qureshi Chooses Reinvention Over ComfortTo play Suman Akka in Sairat, Kadam learnt Telugu from the camera crew on set, while in the case of Fandry, she took lessons in Kaikadi. “It is not enough to learn the lines. You need to know the language to understand the emotion,” she insists.According to Kendre, Kadam had an inferiority complex because of her economic status and language shortcomings. But she overcame those mental blocks and discovered herself as an actor and a human being. “Such a journey is rare. She has marched ahead confidently and has not looked back. I expect her to do a lot more,” he says.Achievements like going to Cannes for All We Imagine as Light are the icing on the cake for Kadam. She does not read too much into it though, saying it is in the past. “I like to live in the moment. I don’t think too far ahead. I just have to do good work,” says the actor, who likes to try various delicacies, travel to her native place in Konkan and spend time with the people closest to her.Conscious of the fact that she has to keep opening new doors for herself, Kadam appears unperturbed even if all this ends one day. “I came here with an empty bag. I have no fear about the bag being empty again,” she says.

title: शी चिनफिंग ने बांग्लादेश के प्रधानमंत्री तारिक रहमान से मुलाकात की
description:
26 जून की सुबह, चीनी राष्ट्रपति शी चिनफिंग ने पेइचिंग में चीन की आधिकारिक यात्रा पर आए बांग्लादेश के प्रधानमंत्री तारिक रहमान से मुलाकात की। दोनों नेताओं ने संयुक्त रूप से नए युग में साझा भविष्य वाले चीन-बांग्लादेश समुदाय के निर्माण और द्विपक्षीय संबंधों को नए एवं उच्च स्तर पर ले जाने की घोषणा की।.
The post शी चिनफिंग ने बांग्लादेश के प्रधानमंत्री तारिक रहमान से मुलाकात की appeared first on Dainik Savera Times.
content:
26 जून की सुबह, चीनी राष्ट्रपति शी चिनफिंग ने पेइचिंग में चीन की आधिकारिक यात्रा पर आए बांग्लादेश के प्रधानमंत्री तारिक रहमान से मुलाकात की। दोनों नेताओं ने संयुक्त रूप से नए युग में साझा भविष्य वाले चीन-बांग्लादेश समुदाय के निर्माण और द्विपक्षीय संबंधों को नए एवं उच्च स्तर पर ले जाने की घोषणा की।
शी चिनफिंग ने कहा कि चीन हमेशा से चीन-बांग्लादेश संबंधों के विकास को बहुत महत्व देता है और बांग्लादेश की जनता के प्रति सद्भावनापूर्ण और मैत्रीपूर्ण नीति पर कायम है। उन्होंने कहा कि अंतरराष्ट्रीय परिस्थितियों में चाहे जितने भी बदलाव आएं, चीन चीन-बांग्लादेश मैत्री की दिशा के प्रति अपनी प्रतिबद्धता पर अडिग रहेगा और बांग्लादेश का विश्वसनीय मित्र, अच्छा पड़ोसी और भरोसेमंद सहयोगी बना रहेगा। चीन, बांग्लादेश की राष्ट्रीय स्वतंत्रता, संप्रभुता और प्रादेशिक अखंडता की रक्षा के प्रयासों का समर्थन करता है और किसी भी बाहरी हस्तक्षेप का विरोध करता है। 
उन्होंने कहा कि चीन, बांग्लादेश के साथ शासन संबंधी अनुभवों का आदान-प्रदान बढ़ाने, सभी स्तरों पर घनिष्ठ संपर्क बनाए रखने, रणनीतिक संवाद को गहरा करने, राजनीतिक आपसी विश्वास मजबूत करने और एक-दूसरे के मूल हितों तथा प्रमुख चिंताओं से जुड़े मुद्दों पर लगातार समर्थन देने के लिए तैयार है।
शी चिनफिंग ने जोर देकर कहा कि चीन पड़ोसी देशों के साथ मैत्री, सुरक्षा और समृद्धि की नीति तथा सौहार्द, सद्भावना, पारस्परिक लाभ और समावेशिता की अवधारणा पर कायम है। उन्होंने कहा कि चीन अपने नए विकास के माध्यम से पड़ोसी देशों के लिए नए अवसर और नई ऊर्जा पैदा करता रहेगा। चीन, बांग्लादेश की नई सरकार के सुचारू संचालन का समर्थन करता है और बेल्ट एंड रोड पहल के उच्च गुणवत्ता वाले सहनिर्माण, विकास रणनीतियों के बेहतर समन्वय, विकास अनुभवों के आदान-प्रदान तथा प्रमुख सहयोग परियोजनाओं की व्यवस्थित योजना पर मिलकर काम करने को तैयार है। 
उन्होंने कहा कि दोनों देश हरित एवं कम कार्बन विकास, डिजिटल अर्थव्यवस्था, सूचना प्रौद्योगिकी और कृत्रिम बुद्धिमत्ता जैसे क्षेत्रों में सहयोग की नई संभावनाएं तलाश सकते हैं। साथ ही चिकित्सा, संस्कृति और शिक्षा के क्षेत्र में आदान-प्रदान बढ़ाने, चीन-म्यांमार-बांग्लादेश आर्थिक गलियारे के निर्माण को आगे बढ़ाने और क्षेत्रीय संपर्क को मजबूत करने के लिए भी दोनों देशों को मिलकर काम करना चाहिए।
तारिक रहमान ने चीनी कम्युनिस्ट पार्टी की 105वीं वर्षगांठ पर बधाई दी। उन्होंने कहा कि चीन एक महान देश है और बांग्लादेश की विदेश नीति में उसका हमेशा महत्वपूर्ण स्थान रहा है। उन्होंने चीन को बांग्लादेश का मूल्यवान और विश्वसनीय साझेदार बताया। उन्होंने खुशी जताई कि दोनों देशों के संबंध नए युग में साझा भविष्य वाले बांग्लादेश-चीन समुदाय के नए स्तर तक पहुंच गए हैं। उन्होंने कहा कि राष्ट्रपति शी चिनफिंग के सशक्त नेतृत्व में चीन ने विकास के क्षेत्र में उल्लेखनीय उपलब्धियां हासिल की हैं और चीनी शैली का आधुनिकीकरण बांग्लादेश के लिए सीखने का एक आदर्श है। 
उन्होंने कहा कि बांग्लादेश, चीन के साथ अंतर-पार्टीय आदान-प्रदान को मजबूत करने, बेल्ट एंड रोड पहल के सह-निर्माण को आगे बढ़ाने तथा व्यापार, कृषि, विज्ञान एवं प्रौद्योगिकी, हरित ऊर्जा, शिक्षा और स्वास्थ्य जैसे क्षेत्रों में सहयोग बढ़ाने का इच्छुक है, ताकि बांग्लादेश के आधुनिकीकरण की प्रक्रिया को गति मिल सके।
(साभार- चाइना मीडिया ग्रुप, पेइचिंग)
The post शी चिनफिंग ने बांग्लादेश के प्रधानमंत्री तारिक रहमान से मुलाकात की appeared first on Dainik Savera Times.

title: EPFO portal unavailable from June 26-30: Check affected facilities to avoid inconvenience
description: EPFO portal unavailable: EPFO expects all services to be fully restored from July 1, 2026. The organisation regrets the temporary inconvenience and appreciates users' patience as it undertakes this important upgrade to deliver faster, more reliable, and more secure services. For any assistance, members can contact the EPFO Call Centre at 14470.
content: EPFO portal unavailable: To enhance service delivery, improve processing efficiency, and provide a better user experience, EPFO is undertaking a planned database consolidation and upgradation of software applications for the claims processing system. The Employees' Provident Fund Organisation's website will be temporarily unavailable from 12:00 am until June 30, 2026, at 11:59 pm. EPFO informed about the same in a pop-up alert on its official portal (https://unifiedportal-emp.epfindia.gov.in/epfo/). According to the alert, titled - 'Important Notice', there will be a Scheduled System Migration and Temporary Service Unavailability. Here's what users must know.According to the EPFO’s official website: “During this period, members and employers will not be able to log in to the Member Interface and Employer Interface. Consequently, all online services available through these interfaces will remain unavailable.”Read more: NFO Alert: 5 mutual funds open for subscription; check key dates, minimum investment amount, NAV, allotment details EPFO Service Interruption Alert: Check list of interrupted services to avoid inconvenienceDuring this period:Submission of new claim requests will be temporarily unavailable.Claim processing services will be temporarily unavailable.Claims already submitted before the migration period will be taken up for processing after services resume.In a social media post on its account on X (formerly known as Twitter), the EPFO announced that: “Services on Member Portal, Employer Portal & UMANG App will remain temporarily unavailable from June 26, 30, 2026, due to scheduled system migration. The upgraded system will deliver faster, more reliable and secure services.” As mentioned above, members will not be able to submit new EPF claims or track the processing of existing claims during the migration period.Employers will be unable to file Electronic Challan-cum-Return (ECR) while the system upgrade is underway.UAN linking for newly joined employees won’t be available during this period.Also, members will not be able to access or download their e-Passbooks until the services are restored.EPFO portal downtime: When will services resume?EPFO expects all services to be fully restored from July 1, 2026. The organisation regrets the temporary inconvenience and appreciates users' patience as it undertakes this important upgrade to deliver faster, more reliable, and more secure services. For any assistance, members can contact the EPFO Call Centre at 14470.Also read: Mutual Fund Returns: Rs 10,000 monthly SIP turned into Rs 44 lakh - Small cap, mid cap, or flexi cap: Which delivered highest 10-Year CAGR - Do you hold?

title: Polymarket Reports $2.9 Million Theft, Says Customers Will Be Refunded
description: Compromised third-party dependency triggered phishing attack on users
content: Prediction market platform Polymarket has confirmed that users affected by a phishing attack stemming from a compromised third-party vendor will be fully refunded. Blockchain analyst Specter estimated that attackers stole nearly $2.94 million worth of cryptocurrency from at least 11 wallets after a malicious script was injected into the platform's website. Polymarket said the affected dependency has been removed and described the incident as isolated.

title: ବଜ୍ରପାତରେ ଚାଲିଗଲା ୨ ଜୀବନ
description:
ବୌଦ୍ଧ,୨୬।୬: ଦକ୍ଷିଣ-ପଶ୍ଚିମ ମୌସୁମୀ ବାୟୁ ପ୍ରଭାବରେ ରାଜ୍ୟର ବିଭିନ୍ନ ଜିଲା ବିଶେଷ କରି ଦକ୍ଷିଣ ଓଡ଼ିଶାରେ ବର୍ଷା ଲାଗି ରହିଛି। ଏପରିକି ଆଞ୍ଚଳିକ ପାଣିପାଗ କେନ୍ଦ୍ର ପକ୍ଷରୁ ଶୁକ୍ରବାର ବୌଦ୍ଧ ଜିଲାରେ ଝଡ଼ବର୍ଷା ହେବା ନେଇ ଆକଳନ କରାଯାଇଥିଲା। ଏହି ପରିପ୍ରେକ୍ଷୀରେ ଅପରାହ୍ନରେ ବୌଦ୍ଧ ଜିଲା ମନମୁଣ୍ଡା ଝାଙ୍କରପଡ଼ା ଅଞ୍ଚଳରେ ହଠାତ କଳାହାଣ୍ଡିଆ ମେଘ ଘୋଟି ଆସି ଝଡ଼ବର୍ଷା ହୋଇଥିଲା। ସେତିକିବେଳେ ୨ଜଣ ଯୁବକ ଏକ ଗଛ ତଳେ ଆଶ୍ରୟ ନେଇଥିବା ବେଳେ ବଜ୍ରପାତ ଘଟି ସେମାନଙ୍କ […]
The post ବଜ୍ରପାତରେ ଚାଲିଗଲା ୨ ଜୀବନ appeared first on Dharitri Odia News.
content:
ବୌଦ୍ଧ,୨୬।୬: ଦକ୍ଷିଣ-ପଶ୍ଚିମ ମୌସୁମୀ ବାୟୁ ପ୍ରଭାବରେ ରାଜ୍ୟର ବିଭିନ୍ନ ଜିଲା ବିଶେଷ କରି ଦକ୍ଷିଣ ଓଡ଼ିଶାରେ ବର୍ଷା ଲାଗି ରହିଛି। ଏପରିକି ଆଞ୍ଚଳିକ ପାଣିପାଗ କେନ୍ଦ୍ର ପକ୍ଷରୁ ଶୁକ୍ରବାର ବୌଦ୍ଧ ଜିଲାରେ ଝଡ଼ବର୍ଷା ହେବା ନେଇ ଆକଳନ କରାଯାଇଥିଲା। ଏହି ପରିପ୍ରେକ୍ଷୀରେ ଅପରାହ୍ନରେ ବୌଦ୍ଧ ଜିଲା ମନମୁଣ୍ଡା ଝାଙ୍କରପଡ଼ା ଅଞ୍ଚଳରେ ହଠାତ କଳାହାଣ୍ଡିଆ ମେଘ ଘୋଟି ଆସି ଝଡ଼ବର୍ଷା ହୋଇଥିଲା। ସେତିକିବେଳେ ୨ଜଣ ଯୁବକ ଏକ ଗଛ ତଳେ ଆଶ୍ରୟ ନେଇଥିବା ବେଳେ ବଜ୍ରପାତ ଘଟି ସେମାନଙ୍କ ମୃତ୍ୟୁ ଘଟିଛି।
ଖବର ପାଇ ପୋଲିସ ଘଟଣାସ୍ଥଳରେ ପହଞ୍ଚି ମୃତଦେହ ଜବତ କରି ତଦନ୍ତ ଆରମ୍ଭ କରିଛି। ଏନେଇ ଗାଁରେ ଶୋକାକୂଳ ପରିବେଶ ସୃଷ୍ଟି ହୋଇଛି।
ସୂଚନା ଯୋଗ୍ୟ, ଝଡ଼ବର୍ଷା ପାଇଁ ସୁନ୍ଦରଗଡ଼, କେନ୍ଦୁଝର, ମୟୂରଭଞ୍ଜ, ନୂଆପଡ଼ା, ବଲାଙ୍ଗୀର, କଳାହାଣ୍ଡି, କନ୍ଧମାଳ, ରାୟଗଡା ଏବଂ ଗଜପତି ଜିଲାକୁ ଅରେଞ୍ଜ ଓ୍ବାର୍ନିଂ ଜାରି କରାଯାଇଛି। ସେହିପରି ବାଲେଶ୍ୱର, ଭଦ୍ରକ, ଯାଜପୁର, କେନ୍ଦ୍ରାପଡ଼ା, ଜଗତ୍ସିଂହପୁର, କଟକ, ଝାରସୁଗୁଡା, ବରଗଡ଼, ସମ୍ବଲପୁର, ଦେବଗଡ଼, ଅନୁଗୋଳ, ଢେଙ୍କାନାଳ, ସୋନପୁର, ବୌଦ୍ଧ, ନବରଙ୍ଗପୁର, କୋରାପୁଟ, ମାଲକାନଗିରି, ଗଞ୍ଜାମ, ପୁରୀ, ଖୋର୍ଦ୍ଧା ଏବଂ ନୟାଗଡ଼ ଜିଲାକୁ ୟେଲୋ ଓ୍ବାର୍ନିଂ ଦିଆଯାଇଛି।
The post ବଜ୍ରପାତରେ ଚାଲିଗଲା ୨ ଜୀବନ appeared first on Dharitri Odia News.

title: ବଢିବ ସିୟାଙ୍କ ସମସ୍ୟା! କସାବକୁ ମୃତ୍ୟୁଦଣ୍ଡ ଦେଇଥିବା ଓକିଲ ଲଢ଼ିବେ କେତନଙ୍କ ହତ୍ୟା ମାମଲା
description:
ପୁଣେ,୨୬।୬: ପୁଣେର ପ୍ରସିଦ୍ଧ ବ୍ୟବସାୟୀ କେତନ ଅଗ୍ରୱାଲଙ୍କ ସନ୍ଦେହଜନକ ମୃତ୍ୟୁ ମାମଲାରେ ଏକ ବଡ଼ ଘଟଣା ସାମ୍ନାକୁ ଆସିଛି। ଏହି ହାଇପ୍ରୋଫାଇଲ ହତ୍ୟାକାଣ୍ଡକୁ ନେଇ ସାରା ମହାରାଷ୍ଟ୍ରରେ ଉତ୍ତେଜନା ମଧ୍ୟରେ, ରାଜ୍ୟ ସରକାର ନ୍ୟାୟିକ ପ୍ରକ୍ରିୟାକୁ ତ୍ୱରାନ୍ବିତ କରିବା ପାଇଁ ପ୍ରସ୍ତୁତ ହୋଇଛନ୍ତି। ଦେଶର ପ୍ରବୀଣ ଏବଂ ବରିଷ୍ଠ ଆଇନଜୀବୀ ଉଜ୍ଜ୍ୱଳ ନିକମ, ଯିଏ ୨୬/୧୧ ମୁମ୍ବାଇ ଆତଙ୍କବାଦୀ ଆକ୍ରମଣର ମୁଖ୍ୟ ଅପରାଧୀ ଅଜମଲ କସାବକୁ ମୃତ୍ୟୁଦଣ୍ଡ ସୁନିଶ୍ଚିତ କରିଥିଲେ, ଏବେ କୋର୍ଟରେ ପୀଡିତଙ୍କ ପକ୍ଷ ଉପସ୍ଥାପନ […]
The post ବଢିବ ସିୟାଙ୍କ ସମସ୍ୟା! କସାବକୁ ମୃତ୍ୟୁଦଣ୍ଡ ଦେଇଥିବା ଓକିଲ ଲଢ଼ିବେ କେତନଙ୍କ ହତ୍ୟା ମାମଲା appeared first on Dharitri Odia News.
content:
ପୁଣେ,୨୬।୬: ପୁଣେର ପ୍ରସିଦ୍ଧ ବ୍ୟବସାୟୀ କେତନ ଅଗ୍ରୱାଲଙ୍କ ସନ୍ଦେହଜନକ ମୃତ୍ୟୁ ମାମଲାରେ ଏକ ବଡ଼ ଘଟଣା ସାମ୍ନାକୁ ଆସିଛି। ଏହି ହାଇପ୍ରୋଫାଇଲ ହତ୍ୟାକାଣ୍ଡକୁ ନେଇ ସାରା ମହାରାଷ୍ଟ୍ରରେ ଉତ୍ତେଜନା ମଧ୍ୟରେ, ରାଜ୍ୟ ସରକାର ନ୍ୟାୟିକ ପ୍ରକ୍ରିୟାକୁ ତ୍ୱରାନ୍ବିତ କରିବା ପାଇଁ ପ୍ରସ୍ତୁତ ହୋଇଛନ୍ତି। ଦେଶର ପ୍ରବୀଣ ଏବଂ ବରିଷ୍ଠ ଆଇନଜୀବୀ ଉଜ୍ଜ୍ୱଳ ନିକମ, ଯିଏ ୨୬/୧୧ ମୁମ୍ବାଇ ଆତଙ୍କବାଦୀ ଆକ୍ରମଣର ମୁଖ୍ୟ ଅପରାଧୀ ଅଜମଲ କସାବକୁ ମୃତ୍ୟୁଦଣ୍ଡ ସୁନିଶ୍ଚିତ କରିଥିଲେ, ଏବେ କୋର୍ଟରେ ପୀଡିତଙ୍କ ପକ୍ଷ ଉପସ୍ଥାପନ କରିବେ।
କେତନ ଅଗ୍ରୱାଲ ହତ୍ୟା ମାମଲାର ଗମ୍ଭୀରତାକୁ ସ୍ବୀକାର କରି, ମହାରାଷ୍ଟ୍ର ସରକାର ଉଜ୍ଜ୍ୱଳ ନିକମଙ୍କୁ ସ୍ବତନ୍ତ୍ର ସରକାରୀ ଓକିଲ ଭାବରେ ନିଯୁକ୍ତ କରିଛନ୍ତି। ବରିଷ୍ଠ ଆଇନଜୀବୀ ନିକମ ଏହି ଗୁରୁତ୍ୱପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣ ଦାୟିତ୍ୱ ଗ୍ରହଣ କରିଛନ୍ତି ଏବଂ ମାମଲା ଲଢ଼ିବାକୁ ରାଜି ହୋଇଛନ୍ତି।
ମହାରାଷ୍ଟ୍ର ମୁଖ୍ୟମନ୍ତ୍ରୀ ଦେବେନ୍ଦ୍ର ଫଡନଭିସ ବ୍ୟକ୍ତିଗତ ଭାବରେ ପରିସ୍ଥିତି ଉପରେ ନଜର ରଖିଛନ୍ତି ଏବଂ ପ୍ରଶାସନକୁ କଡ଼ା ନିର୍ଦ୍ଦେଶ ଦେଇଛନ୍ତି। ମୁଖ୍ୟମନ୍ତ୍ରୀ ସ୍ପଷ୍ଟ କରିଛନ୍ତି ଯେ ପୀଡିତଙ୍କ ପରିବାରକୁ ନ୍ୟାୟ ପ୍ରଦାନ କରିବାରେ କୌଣସି ପ୍ରକାରର ଶିଥିଳତାକୁ ବରଦାସ୍ତ କରାଯିବ ନାହିଁ। ପ୍ରତିଦିନ ଏବଂ ତ୍ୱରିତ ଶୁଣାଣି ସୁନିଶ୍ଚିତ କରିବା ପାଇଁ ସରକାର ମାମଲାର ଫାଷ୍ଟ-ଟ୍ରାକ ବିଚାରକୁ ଅନୁମୋଦନ କରିଛନ୍ତି। ମୁଖ୍ୟମନ୍ତ୍ରୀଙ୍କ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟାଳୟରୁ ଏକ ବିବୃତ୍ତିରେ କୁହାଯାଇଛି ଯେ କେତନ ଅଗ୍ରୱାଲଙ୍କ ହତ୍ୟାକାରୀଙ୍କୁ ନ୍ୟାୟ ପ୍ରଦାନ କରିବା ଏବଂ ସେମାନଙ୍କୁ କଠୋରତମ ଦଣ୍ଡ ପ୍ରଦାନ କରିବା ସରକାରଙ୍କ ପ୍ରାଥମିକତା, ଏବଂ ବିଳମ୍ବ ନକରି ଏହି ଦିଗରେ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟ ଆରମ୍ଭ ହୋଇସାରିଛି।
କେତନ ଅଗ୍ରୱାଲଙ୍କ ପିତା ବିଶାଳ ଅଗ୍ରୱାଲ ଏବଂ ମୁଖ୍ୟମନ୍ତ୍ରୀ ଦେବେନ୍ଦ୍ର ଫଡନାଭିସଙ୍କ ମଧ୍ୟରେ ଏକ ଗୁରୁତ୍ୱପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣ ବୈଠକ ପରେ ଏହି ପ୍ରମୁଖ ନିଷ୍ପତ୍ତି ନିଆଯାଇଛି। ପୁଅର ମୃତ୍ୟୁରେ ଶୋକ ପ୍ରକାଶ କରୁଥିବା ବିଶାଳ ଅଗ୍ରୱାଲ ତାଙ୍କ ଏକମାତ୍ର ପୁଅ ପାଇଁ ଶୀଘ୍ର ନ୍ୟାୟ ପାଇଁ ନିବେଦନ କରିବା ଲାଗି ନିକଟରେ ମୁମ୍ବାଇରେ ମୁଖ୍ୟମନ୍ତ୍ରୀଙ୍କୁ ଭେଟିଥିଲେ।
ବୈଠକ ସମୟରେ, ଭାବପ୍ରବଣ ପିତା ମୁଖ୍ୟମନ୍ତ୍ରୀଙ୍କ ସମ୍ମୁଖରେ ଦୁଇଟି ପ୍ରମୁଖ ଦାବି ରଖିଥିଲେ। ତାଙ୍କର ପ୍ରଥମ ଦାବି ଥିଲା ଯେ, ମାମଲାର ସମ୍ବେଦନଶୀଳତାକୁ ଦୃଷ୍ଟିରେ ରଖି, ଦୋଷୀଙ୍କୁ ଦଣ୍ଡ ଦେବାରେ ବର୍ଷ ବର୍ଷ ବିଳମ୍ବକୁ ରୋକିବା ପାଇଁ ନିୟମିତ କୋର୍ଟ ପରିବର୍ତ୍ତେ ଫାଷ୍ଟ-ଟ୍ରାକ କୋର୍ଟରେ ବିଚାର କରାଯିବା ଉଚିତ। ତାଙ୍କର ଦ୍ୱିତୀୟ ଏବଂ ସବୁଠାରୁ ଗୁରୁତ୍ୱପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣ ଦାବି ଥିଲା ଯେ ମାମଲାର ଦାୟିତ୍ୱ ଦେଶର ସବୁଠାରୁ ଦକ୍ଷ ସରକାରୀ ଓକିଲ ଉଜ୍ଜ୍ୱଳ ନିକମଙ୍କ ଉପରେ ନ୍ୟସ୍ତ କରାଯାଉ। ମୁଖ୍ୟମନ୍ତ୍ରୀ ଦେବେନ୍ଦ୍ର ଫଡନାଭିସ ଆହତ ପିତାଙ୍କ ଦୁଃଖଦ କଥାକୁ ଧ୍ୟାନର ସହିତ ଶୁଣିଲେ ଏବଂ ସମୟ ନଷ୍ଟ ନକରି ତୁରନ୍ତ ତାଙ୍କର ଉଭୟ ଦାବିକୁ ଗ୍ରହଣ କଲେ। ସେ ରାଜ୍ୟ ଆଇନ ଏବଂ ନ୍ୟାୟ ବିଭାଗର ବରିଷ୍ଠ ଅଧିକାରୀମାନଙ୍କୁ ଘଟଣାସ୍ଥଳକୁ ଡକାଇ ଆବଶ୍ୟକୀୟ କାଗଜପତ୍ର ପ୍ରସ୍ତୁତ କରିବାକୁ ଏବଂ ଆବଶ୍ୟକୀୟ ନିର୍ଦ୍ଦେଶ ଜାରି କରିବାକୁ ନିର୍ଦ୍ଦେଶ ଦେଇଥିଲେ। ସରକାରଙ୍କ ଏହି ତ୍ୱରିତ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟାନୁଷ୍ଠାନ ପୀଡିତ ପରିବାରକୁ ଆଶ୍ୱସ୍ତ କରିଛି ଯେ ତାଙ୍କ ପୁଅର ହତ୍ୟାକାରୀମାନେ ଆଇନର ପଞ୍ଝାରୁ ଖସିପାରିବେ ନାହିଁ।
ମୁଖ୍ୟମନ୍ତ୍ରୀ ଦେବେନ୍ଦ୍ର ଫଡନାଭିସ କେତନଙ୍କ ପରିବାରକୁ ଆଶ୍ୱସ୍ତ କରିଛନ୍ତି ଯେ ଏହି ଆଇନଗତ ଲଢ଼େଇରେ ରାଜ୍ୟ ସରକାର ସେମାନଙ୍କ ସହିତ ଦୃଢ଼ ଭାବରେ ଠିଆ ହୋଇଛନ୍ତି। ମାମଲାକୁ ଫାଷ୍ଟ-ଟ୍ରାକ କୋର୍ଟକୁ ସ୍ଥାନାନ୍ତର କରିବାର ପ୍ରାଥମିକ ସୁବିଧା ହେଉଛି ଯେ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟଧାରା ବିନା ବାଧାରେ ଜାରି ରହିବ। ସାଧାରଣତଃ, କୋର୍ଟରେ ମାମଲାଗୁଡ଼ିକର ଲମ୍ବା ଲାଇନ ଯୋଗୁ, ପରିବାରଗୁଡ଼ିକ ପ୍ରାୟତଃ ବର୍ଷ ବର୍ଷ ଧରି ଅଶେଷ ସ୍ଥଗିତ ରହଣିର ସମ୍ମୁଖୀନ ହୁଅନ୍ତି, ଯାହା ସେମାନଙ୍କୁ ମାନସିକ ଭାବରେ ଭାଙ୍ଗି ପକାଏ। ତଥାପି, ଫାଷ୍ଟ-ଟ୍ରାକ କୋର୍ଟଗୁଡ଼ିକ ସାପ୍ତାହିକ ଶୁଣାଣି ପାଇଁ ଅନୁମତି ଦିଅନ୍ତି, ଯାହା ତଦନ୍ତ ଦଳ ଦ୍ୱାରା ଉପସ୍ଥାପିତ ସାକ୍ଷୀ ଏବଂ ପ୍ରମାଣର ଶୀଘ୍ର ଯାଞ୍ଚ ସୁନିଶ୍ଚିତ କରେ। ଆଇନ ବିଶେଷଜ୍ଞମାନେ ବିଶ୍ୱାସ କରନ୍ତି ଯେ ଏହି ପଦକ୍ଷେପ ମାତ୍ର କିଛି ମାସ ମଧ୍ୟରେ କେତନ ଅଗ୍ରୱାଲ ହତ୍ୟା ମାମଲାରେ ରାୟ ଆଣିପାରେ।
ଏହି ମାମଲାରେ ଉଜ୍ଜ୍ୱଳ ନିକମଙ୍କ ପ୍ରବେଶ ଅଭିଯୁକ୍ତଙ୍କ ପାଇଁ ଅସୁବିଧାକୁ ଯଥେଷ୍ଟ ବୃଦ୍ଧି କରିବ। ଉଜ୍ଜ୍ୱଳ ନିକମ ଦେଶର ଆଇନଗତ ଇତିହାସରେ ଜଣେ ଅତ୍ୟନ୍ତ ସମ୍ମାନିତ ବ୍ୟକ୍ତିତ୍ୱ, ଯିଏ ଭାରତର କିଛି ହାଇ-ପ୍ରୋଫାଇଲ ଏବଂ ଜଟିଳ ଅପରାଧିକ ମାମଲାରେ ପ୍ରସିକ୍ୟୁସନର ନେତୃତ୍ୱ ନେଇଥିଲେ। ସେ ୨୦୦୮ ମସିହାରେ ମୁମ୍ବାଇ ଉପରେ ହୋଇଥିବା ଭୟଙ୍କର ଆତଙ୍କବାଦୀ ଆକ୍ରମଣ ମାମଲାରେ ତାଙ୍କର ସର୍ବବୃହତ ସଫଳତା ହାସଲ କରିଥିଲେ; ସେ ପାକିସ୍ତାନୀ ଆତଙ୍କବାଦୀ ଅଜମଲ କସାବଙ୍କ ବିରୁଦ୍ଧରେ ଏପରି ଆକର୍ଷଣୀୟ ଏବଂ ଅକାଟ୍ୟ ଯୁକ୍ତି ଉପସ୍ଥାପନ କରିଥିଲେ ଯେ କୋର୍ଟ ପାଖରେ ତାଙ୍କୁ ମୃତ୍ୟୁଦଣ୍ଡ ଦେବା ବ୍ୟତୀତ ଅନ୍ୟ କୌଣସି ବିକଳ୍ପ ନଥିଲା। ଏହା ବ୍ୟତୀତ, ନିକମ ଅନେକ ବଡ଼ ହତ୍ୟା ମାମଲା ଏବଂ ଅଣ୍ଡରୱାଲର୍‌ଡ ସହିତ ଜଡିତ ମାମଲା ସଫଳତାର ସହ ପରିଚାଳନା କରିଛନ୍ତି।

title: ଅନଶନକାରୀଙ୍କ ସହ ଜିଲାପାଳ-ଏସ୍‌ପିଙ୍କ ରୁଦ୍ଧଦ୍ୱାର ବୈଠକ, ଫଳାଫଳ ଉପରେ ସମସ୍ତଙ୍କ ନଜର
description:
ପଟ୍ଟାମୁଣ୍ଡାଇ,୨୬ା୬(କଳ୍ପତରୁ ନାୟକ): ପଟ୍ଟାମୁଣ୍ଡାଇ ଜଳସମ୍ପଦ ବିଭାଗ ଅଳଭା-୨ ଅଧୀନ ୧୬ନଂ କେନାଲ ରାସ୍ତା କାର୍ଯ୍ୟ ପାଇଁ ଆସିଥିବା ଅର୍ଥ ଆଦୌ ବିନିଯୋଗ ନ ହୋଇ ମିଥ୍ୟା ବିଲ୍‌ କରି ଅର୍ଥ ହଡ଼ପ କରାଯାଇଥିବା ଅଭିଯୋଗ କରି ଶାଶନ ପଞ୍ଚାୟତର ପ୍ରଶାନ୍ତ ମହାନ୍ତି, ଧ୍ରୁବ ପାଣ୍ଡବ, ରମାକାନ୍ତ ଦାଶ ଓ ଅର୍ଜୁନ ସାହୁ ସମେତ କେତେଜଣ ଅଧିବାସୀ ସୋମବାର ପୂର୍ବାହ୍ନରୁ ଜଳସମ୍ପଦ ବିଭାଗ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟାଳୟ ସମ୍ମୁଖରେ ଆମରଣ ଅନଶନରେ ବସିଥିବା ବେଳେ ଅନଶନକୁ ୫ମ ଦିନରେ ପହଞ୍ଚିଛି । […]
The post ଅନଶନକାରୀଙ୍କ ସହ ଜିଲାପାଳ-ଏସ୍‌ପିଙ୍କ ରୁଦ୍ଧଦ୍ୱାର ବୈଠକ, ଫଳାଫଳ ଉପରେ ସମସ୍ତଙ୍କ ନଜର appeared first on Dharitri Odia News.
content:
ପଟ୍ଟାମୁଣ୍ଡାଇ,୨୬ା୬(କଳ୍ପତରୁ ନାୟକ): ପଟ୍ଟାମୁଣ୍ଡାଇ ଜଳସମ୍ପଦ ବିଭାଗ ଅଳଭା-୨ ଅଧୀନ ୧୬ନଂ କେନାଲ ରାସ୍ତା କାର୍ଯ୍ୟ ପାଇଁ ଆସିଥିବା ଅର୍ଥ ଆଦୌ ବିନିଯୋଗ ନ ହୋଇ ମିଥ୍ୟା ବିଲ୍‌ କରି ଅର୍ଥ ହଡ଼ପ କରାଯାଇଥିବା ଅଭିଯୋଗ କରି ଶାଶନ ପଞ୍ଚାୟତର ପ୍ରଶାନ୍ତ ମହାନ୍ତି, ଧ୍ରୁବ ପାଣ୍ଡବ, ରମାକାନ୍ତ ଦାଶ ଓ ଅର୍ଜୁନ ସାହୁ ସମେତ କେତେଜଣ ଅଧିବାସୀ ସୋମବାର ପୂର୍ବାହ୍ନରୁ ଜଳସମ୍ପଦ ବିଭାଗ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟାଳୟ ସମ୍ମୁଖରେ ଆମରଣ ଅନଶନରେ ବସିଥିବା ବେଳେ ଅନଶନକୁ ୫ମ ଦିନରେ ପହଞ୍ଚିଛି । ଯାହାକୁ ନେଇ ଜିଲା ପ୍ରଶାସନର ମୁଣ୍ଡ ବିନ୍ଧାର କାରଣ ପାଲଟିଛି।ଯାହାକୁ ନେଇ ଶୁକ୍ରବାର ଜିଲାପାଳ ଓ ଏସ୍‌ପି ଅଶନକାରୀଙ୍କ ସହ ବ୍ଲକ ସମ୍ମିଳନୀ କକ୍ଷରେ ରୁଦ୍ଧଦ୍ୱାର ବୈଠକ କରିଛନ୍ତି। ଅନଶନକାରୀଙ୍କ ଦାବି ଓ ସମସ୍ୟା ନେଇ ବିସ୍ତୃତ ଆଲୋଚନା ହୋଇଥିବା ସୂଚନା ମିଳିଛି। ପ୍ରଶାସନ ପକ୍ଷରୁ ଶାନ୍ତିପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣ ଓ ଆଲୋଚନା ମାଧ୍ୟମରେ ସମାଧାନ ପାଇଁ ପ୍ରୟାସ ଜାରି ରହିଛି। ବୈଠକ ପୂର୍ବରୁ ଉଭୟଙ୍କ ପକ୍ଷରୁ କୌଣସି ବିବୃତ୍ତି ଦିଆଯାଇନାହିଁ। ତେବେ ଆଲୋଚନା ସକାରାତ୍ମକ ପରିବେଶରେ ହୋଇଥିବା ଜଣାପଡ଼ିଛି। ବୈଠକର ଫଳାଫଳ ଉପରେ ସମସ୍ତଙ୍କ ନଜର ରହିଛି।

Dharitri – Odisha’s No.1 Odia Daily

The post ଅନଶନକାରୀଙ୍କ ସହ ଜିଲାପାଳ-ଏସ୍‌ପିଙ୍କ ରୁଦ୍ଧଦ୍ୱାର ବୈଠକ, ଫଳାଫଳ ଉପରେ ସମସ୍ତଙ୍କ ନଜର appeared first on Dharitri Odia News.

title: OnePlus N6 Display, Camera Configuration and Other Key Details Confirmed Days Before India Launch
description: OnePlus N6 will feature an 8-megapixel selfie camera
content: OnePlus N6 will be launched in India with a flat display that refreshes at up to 120Hz and offers up to 1,200 nits peak brightness, the smartphone maker revealed on Friday. Moreover, the smartphone will feature a dual rear camera system, headlined by a 50-megapixel primary shooter and an unspecified secondary camera. On the front, the OnePlus N6 will be equipped with an 8-megapixel camera for selfies and video calls. The company claims that the handset will be capable of recording videos at up to 60 fps, while also offering Dual-View video mode. For thermal management, the OnePlus N6 will launch equipped with a vapour chamber cooling solution, offering a 5,300 sq mm heat dissipation area.

title: Why Apple Finally Raised Prices And Why Other Brands Did It First
description: From Apple to Samsung and Nothing, every brand is hiking prices. Here's why AI's growing appetite for memory chips affects your wallet.
content: Your favourite phone brand just hiked its prices. So did the one you switched from. And the one before that. Apple, Samsung, OnePlus, Nothing, Vivo, iQOO, Realme, pick any name you like, the story's the same. Something quietly expensive happened to the insides of every gadget you own and no one's talking about it loudly enough. Here's the short version: the memory chips inside your phone and laptop have quietly turned into gold. And the longer version, which actually explains why this is happening, who's responsible and why today might genuinely be the best day to buy a gadget, is a whole lot more interesting.RAM Is Fuel And We're Running Out Of ItThink of RAM like petrol in a car. Without enough of it, your phone stutters, apps crash and multitasking becomes a fantasy. Now imagine that petrol suddenly became scarce, not because we ran out of oil, but because a much bigger, much richer customer started buying up every barrel available. That's more or less what's happening. The two main types of memory inside consumer devices- DRAM (the 'RAM' in your phone) and NAND flash (the storage), are being consumed at a scale the industry has never seen before. The culprit? Artificial intelligence. Training a large language model or running AI inference at scale requires enormous quantities of a specialised memory type called High Bandwidth Memory, or HBM. Building AI servers means loading them up with HBM stacks and the three companies that make almost all the world's memory chips (Samsung, SK Hynix and Micron) have limited factory space. Every wafer they dedicate to HBM for an Nvidia AI accelerator is a wafer that won't become the LPDDR5X RAM inside your next mid-range phone.According to am analysis by IDC, this isn't a standard boom-and-bust cycle. It's a strategic reallocation, hyperscalers like Microsoft, Google and Amazon are signing long-term contracts for memory at premium prices, effectively outbidding every smartphone or PC brand trying to secure chips. The result, as IDC puts it, is a zero-sum game: what goes to AI data centres doesn't go to you. IDC also projects that DRAM supply growth in 2026 will come in at just 16% year-on-year, well below historical norms, while NAND won't fare much better at 17%. In a market where demand is surging and supply is crawling, prices only go one way.      Apple Held Out Longer Than Everyone ElseApple was actually the last major brand to raise prices, which is worth saying out loud because the headlines make it sound otherwise. The Cupertino-based tech giant had been absorbing cost pressure through long-term supply agreements and its massive cash reserves for months, while everyone else had already started revising price tags upward. When it finally moved, it moved pretty hard. Apple's price hikes in India were significantly steeper than in the US, often by double-digit percentages. And it wasn't just the starting prices that changed. Apple also revised what it charges for memory and storage upgrades from its online store, bumping increments from Rs 20,000 to Rs 24,000 (with higher storage tiers going up in Rs 30,000 steps). Apple's statement to the media acknowledged that the news wasn't welcome, though the company offered no timeline for relief. Here's a look at some of the most eye-opening Apple India price changes:ModelPrevious Price (INR)Current Price (INR)Hike (%)MacBook Air 13 (16GB/512GB)Rs 1,19,900Rs 1,49,90025%MacBook Pro 14 (16GB/1TB)Rs 1,89,900Rs 2,39,90026.3%iMac (16GB/256GB)Rs 1,34,900Rs 1,74,90029.7%Mac mini (16GB/256GB)Rs 59,900Rs 94,90058.4%iPad Pro 11 (256GB)Rs 99,900Rs 1,39,90040%iPad Air 11 (128GB)Rs 64,900Rs 89,90038.5%iPad (128GB)Rs 34,900Rs 49,90043%HomePod miniRs 10,900Rs 15,90045.9%Apple TV 4K (64GB)Rs 14,900Rs 25,90074%        The Mac mini's entry-level configuration went up by 58.4%. The base iPad jumped 43%. The HomePod mini, a speaker, not a flagship phone, climbed nearly 46%. These aren't rounding adjustments. They're structural changes driven by component costs that Apple simply couldn't absorb indefinitely.Every Brand In India Has Already Done ItLet's be clear: this isn't an Apple problem. It's an everybody problem. Look at the data across the Indian smartphone market and the pattern is impossible to miss. The iQOO Neo 10 went from Rs 31,999 to Rs 41,999- a Rs 10,000 jump. OnePlus 15R added Rs 7,000 to its price tag. Even Nothing, which built its brand on value, raised the Phone (4a) Pro by Rs 5,000. Budget and mid-range brands are actually the most vulnerable in this squeeze. OEMs like Realme, Xiaomi, and iQOO operate on thin margins. For them, absorbing higher memory costs simply isn't an option, so either the price goes up, or the specs get quietly downgraded. Often, it's both.Here's the full picture across brands:BrandSmartphoneVariantPrevious PriceCurrent PricePrice HikeSamsungGalaxy A17 5G8GB+256GBRs 23,499Rs 26,499+₹3,000SamsungGalaxy M368GB+256GBRs 24,499Rs 25,999+₹1,500OnePlusOnePlus 1512GB+256GBRs 72,999Rs 77,999+₹5,000OnePlusOnePlus 15R12GB+256GBRs 47,999Rs 54,999+₹7,000NothingPhone (4a) Pro8GB+128GBRs 39,999Rs 44,999+₹5,000NothingPhone (4a)8GB+128GBRs 31,999Rs 34,999+₹3,000OppoReno 15 Pro12GB+256GBRs 67,999Rs 69,999+₹2,000OppoReno 15 Pro Mini12GB+256GBRs 59,999Rs 62,999+₹3,000Vivovivo V708GB+256GBRs 45,999Rs 49,999+₹4,000Vivovivo Y4008GB+256GBRs 21,999Rs 28,999+₹7,000Xiaomi/RedmiRedmi Note 15 Pro+12GB+256GBRs 37,999Rs 39,999+₹2,000Xiaomi/RedmiRedmi Note 15 Pro8GB+256GBRs 29,999Rs 31,999+₹2,000RealmeRealme 16 Pro+12GB+256GBRs 39,999Rs 46,999+₹7,000RealmeRealme 16 Pro8GB+256GBRs 33,999Rs 39,999+₹6,000iQOOiQOO 1512GB+256GBRs 69,999Rs 74,999+₹5,000iQOOiQOO Neo 108GB+256GBRs 31,499Rs 41,999+₹10,000Premium brands like Apple and Samsung had an edge; long-term memory supply contracts and bigger balance sheets mean they can absorb some of the shock. But even they've hit a wall now. The shortage is freezing spec progression across the board.Is There Any Relief Coming? Not Anytime SoonThe uncomfortable answer is: no, not really. Not this year and maybe not next year either. Building a new memory fabrication plant takes years and costs billions. After a brutal downturn in 2022-23, manufacturers aren't rushing to expand capacity, they got burned last time. Even the expansions that Samsung and SK Hynix have announced won't come online until late 2027 and a large chunk of that new capacity is already earmarked for enterprise and AI customers, not consumer electronics.Micron has now stepped back from its Crucial consumer memory brand, not because demand dried up, but because the opportunity cost of serving everyday consumers is now too high compared to shipping HBM to a data centre. That's how tilted the market has become.IDC's analysis projects that in a moderate downside scenario, the global smartphone market could contract by around 2.9% in 2026, with average selling prices rising 3–5%. In a more pessimistic scenario, those numbers get worse, a 5.2% market contraction and price rises of 6–8%. Laptop prices from Dell, HP, Lenovo and ASUS have already been flagged to rise 15–20% as memory costs hit PC bill-of-materials hard.        The honest takeaway for anyone planning an upgrade: now is better than later. The era of cheap, abundant memory, the kind that quietly made every phone better and every PC more affordable year after year, is on pause. AI needed it more. And until supply catches up with that demand, the bill lands with you.        There's a certain irony buried in all of this. AI was supposed to make technology smarter, faster and more accessible. Instead, the infrastructure needed to build AI has made the technology we already rely on smartphones, laptops, tablets, more expensive and harder to upgrade. The same chips that let ChatGPT answer your questions are the reason your next phone costs Rs 10,000 more than it did six months ago. None of this is likely to reverse quickly. If you've been holding off on a purchase, the calculus has shifted. Today's price might actually be the deal. The real culprit isn't greed. It's silicon.

title: Vivo Y6a Launched With 7,200mAh Battery, 50-Megapixel Rear Camera: Price, Specifications
description: Vivo Y6a features an 8-megapixel selfie camera
content: Vivo Y6a has been launched in China as the tech firm’s latest Y series handset. The new Y series phone is on sale via the Vivo China online store in Galaxy Silver, Obsidian Black, and Phoenix Welcomes Gold (translated from Chinese) colour options.  The handset sports a 6.75-inch HD+ (720 x 1,570 pixels) LCD touchscreen, offering up to 120Hz refresh rate, up to 1,200 nits peak brightness, 256 ppi pixel density, 240Hz touch sampling rate, 83 percent NTSC, and 16.7 million colours. The company claims that the phone ships with IP68 + IP69 ratings for dust and water resistance. It features a Snapdragon 4 Gen 2 chipset.

title: SRK's manager Pooja once managed Deepika, helped her land her first film

title: Vande Mataram not just song, but mantra for reconstruction of India: Amit Shah
description:
New Delhi, Jun 26: Union Home Minister Amit Shah on Friday said 'Vande Mataram' is not merely a patriotic song but a "mantra for the reconstruction of India", as he paid tributes to Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay on his birth anniversary while addressing a meeting on the country's anti-narcotics strategy.
At the 10th apex-level meeting of the Narco-Coordination Centre (NCORD), Shah stated that June 26 is a significant day for the country's campaign against narcotics.
He said it also marks the birth anniversary of Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay, who played a key role in awakening the nation's self-confidence during British rule.
"Bankim Babu composed 'Vande Mataram'. 'Vande Mataram' became a powerful symbol of the nation's cultural consciousness and patriotism. When the freedom struggle reached its peak, the main slogan of the fight against the British was 'Vande Mataram'," Shah said.
Recalling the sacrifices made by freedom fighters, he said many revolutionaries embraced the gallows with the chant of 'Vande Mataram'.
"'Vande Mataram' is not just a slogan or a song for all of us Indians. 'Vande Mataram' is not merely a song that introduced the freedom movement, either. 'Vande Mataram' is a mantra for the reconstruction of India and also a means to re-establish Mother India at the highest position in the world," Shah said.
The meeting brought together representatives of 44 central ministries and departments, state governments and drug law enforcement agencies in a hybrid mode to deliberate on strengthening the government's efforts to achieve a drug-free India.
The discussions focused on tackling the growing menace of synthetic drugs, curbing darknet-enabled trafficking and expanding the reach of rehabilitation centres for drug users over the next three years.

title: Kospi Crash Today: South Korea market's trading halted for second time as AI, chip stocks sink
description: South Korea's KOSPI triggered its second trading halt this week after a sharp sell-off in AI and semiconductor stocks. Samsung and SK Hynix led losses, raising concerns over global market sentiment.
content: South Korea’s benchmark stock index, the KOSPI, witnessed another sharp decline on Friday, forcing the exchange to temporarily halt trading for the second time this week as a wave of selling swept through technology and semiconductor stocks amid mounting concerns over the artificial intelligence (AI) sector.The Korea Exchange suspended trading for 20 minutes after the benchmark index fell beyond the threshold that triggers a circuit breaker. During the session, the KOSPI tumbled as much as 9 per cent before trimming some of its losses. It was last seen trading around 5.8 per cent lower after hitting an intraday low of 8,126.84.The latest sell-off comes just days after the index suffered a massive 10 per cent fall on June 23, marking one of its steepest single-day declines in history. Over the week, the KOSPI has lost nearly 19 per cent, highlighting growing nervousness among investors.Semiconductor giants drag the market lowerThe biggest pressure came from South Korea's leading chipmakers, Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix, both of which plunged more than 10 per cent at one stage during Friday's session.Together, the two companies make up close to 60 per cent of the KOSPI's overall market weight, making their share price movements a key driver of the benchmark's performance.Foreign investors also intensified their selling, offloading stocks worth nearly 5 trillion won, or around Rs 3.1 lakh crore, during the trading session.Given Samsung and SK Hynix's critical role in the global semiconductor supply chain, their steep losses also dampened investor confidence across broader Asian markets.Apple, Microsoft price hikes raise fresh worriesInvestor sentiment weakened further after Apple announced price increases for several products, including iPads and MacBooks, citing rising memory chip costs.The move sparked concerns that higher semiconductor prices could eventually reduce consumer demand for electronic devices. Apple shares dropped 6.1 per cent overnight, wiping out nearly $250 billion in market value.Adding to market concerns, Microsoft also raised prices for Xbox gaming consoles in several countries, reinforcing fears that rising technology costs are increasingly being passed on to consumers.The negative sentiment overshadowed strong quarterly earnings from memory chip manufacturer Micron Technology, whose shares jumped nearly 16 per cent overnight to an all-time high.Why global markets are watching South KoreaSouth Korea is one of the world's largest memory chip producers, making its stock market highly sensitive to developments in the semiconductor industry.As major suppliers to leading global technology companies, Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix have a significant influence on investor sentiment across Asian markets. Sharp declines in their shares often spill over into regional equities, including India.What it could mean for Indian marketsThe steep correction in South Korea's KOSPI may lead to near-term volatility in Indian equities by weakening global risk appetite and encouraging foreign institutional investors (FIIs) to reduce exposure to emerging markets.Technology stocks in India could face pressure as investors reassess global demand for semiconductors and AI-related businesses. Other sectors such as metals, real estate and PSU banks may also experience temporary weakness if risk-off sentiment persists.Despite these global headwinds, India's market fundamentals remain relatively strong. Robust domestic consumption, continued government infrastructure spending, healthy corporate earnings and consistent inflows from domestic institutional investors (DIIs) are expected to provide support and help cushion the broader market from deeper declines.

READ MORE: ‘Jackass: Best And Last’ review: silly stunts and fond farewells

The fifth and final Jackass movie release is in cinemas today, and at the movie’s premiere a drone display paid tribute to various classic moments in the franchise. This included the iconic Jackass emblem of a skull and two crutches, Johnny Knoxville being hit by a giant hand, a portable toilet exploding, and a man bent over with the word “FART” written above him, with fireworks depicting the lit gas. You can check out the full display below.
Originally running for just three seasons between 2000 and 2001, Jackass featured a regular crew of friends performing physically dangerous, comedic, or grotesque stunts. The appeal of the show led to the rise of castmembers such as Johnny Knoxville and Steve-O, and five movies starting with 2002’s Jackass The Movie.

The ‘JACKASS: BEST AND LAST’ world premiere had a drone show featuring iconic stunts from the franchise and a giant fart in the sky. pic.twitter.com/ZqHVQOG38c
— DiscussingFilm (@DiscussingFilm) June 26, 2026

There have also been four straight-to-video alternative cuts for each film, the spin-off Bad Grandpa, and various TV specials. Members of the Jackass crew have starred in their own series, such as Viva La Bam (starring Bam Margera), and Wildboyz, a nature-focused show starring Steve-O and Chris Pontius.
NME gave Jackass: First And Last a three-star review, concluding: “With ‘My Way’ playing, Knoxville, the leader of these merry pranksters, looks misty-eyed as he’s confronted by the fact this chapter of his life is over. As the film takes a bow with one last gross-out moment – a foul-smelling remix on Twister – your eyes will be watering too.
Jackass regular Bam Margera also returned to the franchise via archive footage after missing out on the fourth film due to a legal disagreement with the creators. Last year, Margera said in an interview: “You couldn’t offer me enough money to want to do another Jackass with them. The damage has been done.”
The post ‘Jackass: Best And Last’ premiere featured a giant drone fart in the sky appeared first on NME.

title: Indonesia deactivates 4.7 million TikTok, YouTube accounts of children under 16; broader impact of island nation’s bold move
description: TikTok and YouTube have deactivated a combined 4.7 million accounts belonging to children under 16 in Indonesia since the country introduced stricter online child protection regulations, Communications and Digital Affairs Minister Meutya Hafid said.
content: In a landmark enforcement of its child protection rules, Indonesia has forced major social media platforms to take decisive action. On June 26, 2026, Communications and Digital Minister Meutya Hafid announced that TikTok and YouTube have collectively deactivated approximately 4.7 million accounts belonging to users under the age of 16. TikTok alone removed 4.1 million accounts, while YouTube deactivated 600,000.This development marks a significant milestone in the implementation of Indonesia’s strict new regulations aimed at shielding minors from the darker sides of the digital world.The Policy: PP Tunas and the Under-16 BanThe mass deactivations stem from Government Regulation No. 17/2025 (popularly known as PP Tunas), which governs electronic system providers for child protection. A key ministerial regulation (No. 9 of 2026) signed in early March 2026 designated certain platforms as “high-risk” and mandated the deactivation of accounts for children under 16, with enforcement beginning on March 28, 2026.Affected platforms initially included YouTube, TikTok, Facebook, Instagram, Threads, X (formerly Twitter), Bigo Live, and Roblox. The government cited serious risks: exposure to online pornography, cyberbullying, scams, violent content, and digital addiction. With an estimated 70 million children under 16 in Indonesia, the scale of the challenge is enormous.Indonesia positioned itself as the first non-Western country and the first in Southeast Asia to implement such a sweeping age-based restriction on social media access.Why now? Growing global concernIndonesia’s move did not happen in isolation. It follows Australia’s pioneering 2025 ban on social media for under-16s, which has been closely monitored worldwide. Countries like Britain are now considering broader restrictions that extend to gaming and live-streaming. Indonesia’s regulators explicitly drew inspiration from these efforts while tailoring the policy to local realities.Minister Hafid emphasized that the goal goes beyond merely delaying access: “We’re not just delaying a child’s access, but we want behaviours from platforms to change, too.” The ministry is actively reviewing self-assessment reports from companies and expects more platforms to comply fully.Platform responses and implementationTikTok (ByteDance): Progressively deactivating accounts, with numbers climbing from hundreds of thousands in April to 4.1 million by late June. The company has engaged directly with the government.YouTube (Google/Alphabet): Began phasing out underage accounts and stopped serving ads targeted at children. Users were given opportunities to download their data before deactivation.Other platforms like Meta (Instagram and Facebook) have also signaled compliance. Platforms must now implement stronger age verification, restrict content, and provide parental oversight tools.Human and practical impactFor millions of Indonesian families, this is transformative. Many parents have welcomed the policy, relieved from the constant battle against algorithms designed to maximize screen time. Teachers and child psychologists have long warned about rising cases of anxiety, sleep disruption, and exposure to inappropriate content among young users.However, challenges remain. Critics point to potential issues with age verification accuracy, the risk of driving children toward unregulated VPNs or alternative apps, and the loss of educational value that platforms like YouTube can offer. Enforcement in a vast archipelago nation with uneven digital infrastructure is no small feat.There are also economic ripples. Indonesia is a massive market for short-form video and social media creators, many of whom rely on young audiences. The long-term effects on the creator economy and digital advertising remain to be seen.Global Precedent?Indonesia’s aggressive stance adds fuel to an intensifying international debate: In the rush to connect the world, have platforms outpaced society’s ability to protect its most vulnerable? By taking a firm regulatory line rather than relying solely on self-regulation, Indonesia is testing whether governments can successfully push back against the attention economy. Success here could inspire similar measures across the Global South, where smartphone penetration has surged faster than parental awareness or robust legal frameworks. As the dust settles on this first major wave of deactivations, one thing is clear: the era of unrestricted social media access for children is ending in Indonesia. Whether this bold experiment will genuinely improve young lives  or simply shift the problem elsewhere will be watched closely not just in Jakarta, but in capitals around the world.For now, 4.7 million accounts represent more than numbers on a spreadsheet. They symbolize a nation drawing a line in the digital sand to reclaim childhood in the age of endless scrolls.

title: Foo Fighters live in Liverpool: rock club vibes on a stadium scale from our greatest supergroup
description:
Anfield Stadium, June 25, 2026: Dave Grohl and co bring all the hits and brotherly love for the "cocktail party before the big dinner” as they tease more to come in 2027
The post Foo Fighters live in Liverpool: rock club vibes on a stadium scale from our greatest supergroup appeared first on NME.
content:
“You guys wanna sing a song with me?” Dave Grohl asks the sun-kissed crowd at the first of two sold-out gigs at Anfield, already drenched in beer, sweat, and god-knows-what. “You guys wanna sing 30 songs with me? It’s gonna be a long night motherfuckers.”

READ MORE: Foo Fighters – ‘Your Favorite Toy’ review: back to brawny basics

He’s not wrong. You pretty much know what you’re going to get at a Foo Fighters show, and they always put the hours in to play “aaaaaaaaaaall night”. Along with mammoth sets, they also ladle on the hits and the brotherly love – two things they famously love up in Liverpool.
While still obviously massive, Anfield feels somewhat more intimate than other stadiums (here’s a 60,000-strong crowd compared to Wembley’s 90,000), but Grohl and the band do their utmost to pull everyone to their bosom in an escapable bear hug. Fitting red flares fill up the air from the top of the stage as the band step through the crimson mist with Grohl pegging it down the short gangway before the brutal opening one-two punch of ‘All My Life’ into ‘The Pretender’. “I’ve got a question: do you motherfuckers love rock’n’roll?” he asks. Go on then, and ‘Times Like These’ makes it three.

  










View this post on Instagram

























They wouldn’t be on the road without a reason, mind, and new number ‘Of All People’ from the recent ‘Your Favorite Toy’ turns the dial up to gnarly, but still can’t quite find a deserving home in the hearts of many chattering casual fans. They know how to counter it, mind. “Who’s old school?” asks Grohl before the early stoner rock banger ‘Stacked Actors’, seamlessly rolls into ultimate crowd pleaser ‘My Hero’, the grunge meets Tom Petty grace of ‘Learn To Fly’ and mass sing-along of ‘These Days’. It’s relentless, like being trapped in a bar with a malfunctioning jukebox.
That laid-back rock club vibe is what makes this work. Foos don’t have the bells, whistles, pyro, or blockbuster special effects of other standard stadium acts, just tunes and banter. Who’d have thought that ‘This Is A Call’, ‘For All the Cows’ and ‘Big Me’ – written for their 1995 debut and for sweaty little rooms – would land like Queen classics on this pitch? They keep the backroom jam energy up by splicing a little Motörhead into the Metallica-meets-Sabbath chug-along of ‘No Son Of Mine’ before bringing the vast venue in for a run of numbers on the B-stage, peaking with the tale of writing Nirvana B-side ‘Marigold’ on Kurt Cobain’s couch, and the tender ‘Aurora’ being dedicated to the late, great Taylor Hawkins.
Then they really bring it home medley of covers from each members’ past bands (No Use For A Name, Sunny Day Real Estate, The Germs, to name just a few of them) and with Grohl and drummer Ilan Rubin swapping instruments for a laugh. It’s worth remembering that Foos are much more than “Dave Grohl’s other band” but a pretty mighty supergroup.
Foo Fighters live at Anfield Stadium, Liverpool, 2026. Credit: Andi K Taylor
Our intrigue is piqued when Grohl picks up a young chap from the audience for the challenge of completing a Rubik’s Cube on stage for the privilege of drumming ‘Rope’ with the band. He smashes it, then the illusion is shattered when we learn it’s actually the boyfriend of Grohl’s daughter Harper, and today is his 18th birthday. After an admirably out there encore of the psych-y meandering of ‘The Teacher’ and early deep cut ‘Exhausted’, the staple closing banger ‘Everlong’ and the red fireworks lighting up the sky make everyone in Liverpool feel like it’s their special day.
It all feels a little too familiar to the usual Foos fare, but Radiohead they are not. This is what they do. Grohl asks why oh why the band are only playing these two Liverpool gigs in their home away from home of the UK this year, but assures us that this is merely  “a cocktail party before the big dinner” – with talk of some big plans in Blighty for 2027. For now, “this is just the first date”, so pucker up for Saturday and party on.
Foo Fighters live at Anfield Stadium, Liverpool, 2026. Credit: Andi K Taylor
Foo Fighters live at Anfield Stadium, Liverpool, 2026. Credit: Andi K Taylor

title: Parliamentary panel calls on Ladakh LG, discusses region's key challenges
description:
Leh, Jun 26: The Parliamentary Standing Committee on External Affairs called on Ladakh Lieutenant Governor (LG) V K Saxena and held discussions on the region's key challenges, including environmental concerns, water scarcity and border security.
The committee, chaired by Congress MP Shashi Tharoor, met the lieutenant governor (LG) on Thursday. The panel was on a four-day tour of Jammu and Kashmir, and Ladakh, which began on Monday, to review strategic border security and foreign policy issues.
"Had an engaging and fruitful interaction with the Parliamentary Standing Committee on External Affairs, chaired by MP Dr Shashi Tharoor, during their Ladakh visit," Saxena said in a post on X on Friday.
According to the LG, the committee sought details about Ladakh's major challenges and the measures being taken to address them.
Saxena said he briefed the panel about the fragile ecosystem of Ladakh, environmental challenges and the growing water scarcity in the region.
He informed the committee about initiatives such as Project Him Sarovar, construction of rock check dams under the Sindhu Jal Samridhi Abhiyan and large-scale plantation drives aimed at mitigating these challenges.
"The committee appreciated these efforts and assured full cooperation," the LG said.
The parliamentary panel is visiting the strategically important region to review India-China relations, India-Pakistan ties and the overall security situation along the borders.
During its tour, the parliamentary committee visited Jammu, Srinagar, Kargil and Leh to gain first-hand insights into border management and security in frontier areas.
Earlier this month, members of the 8th Central Pay Commission had also visited Leh to interact with employees' unions and address grievances of the Union territory administration employees.

title: Exclusive: Framework Ventures raises $400 million for fourth fund as firm expands beyond crypto
description: The VC joins a growing list of crypto investors moving beyond digital assets.
content:
Crypto is boring right now, and AI is hot, so it’s no wonder that a growing list of digital asset VCs are expanding their focus beyond blockchain. San Francisco-based Framework Ventures is the latest investor to join the trend, and the firm announced on Friday that it raised $400 million for its fourth fund.



Framework cofounders Vance Spencer and Michael Anderson declined to name their LPs but said contributors to their fourth stash of capital include funds of funds, an Ivy League endowment, sovereign wealth funds, and nonprofits. The VC held $1.28 billion in assets under management in December 2025, according to a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission.



The firm plans to use its new capital to invest in what Spencer and Anderson called “frontier technology,” a sweeping term that not only includes crypto but also AI, robotics, and energy, said the duo. While it may seem like Framework is jumping on the AI bandwagon, the VC is actually following the shifting interests of its network of founders, said Anderson.



“We can see these founders leading us in this direction,” he added. “We should pay attention.” 



DeFi and beyond



Although crypto boomed shortly after President Donald Trump won the election, the market has petered out over the past eight months as Bitcoin approaches lows not seen since 2024. Meanwhile, AI companies like Anthropic and OpenAI continue to balloon in valuation as VCs hope for historic returns when and if the leading AI labs IPO.



Amid the crypto downturn and AI boom, Framework’s competitors have shifted focus. Paradigm, one of the largest VCs in digital assets, is reportedly raising as much as $1.5 billion for a new fund that will not only focus on crypto but also AI and robotics. And Haun Ventures, founded by a former partner at Andreessen Horowitz’s crypto arm, has also expanded its mandate, raising $1 billion for a second fund that targets not only blockchain but also AI, financial services, and alternative assets.



Launched in 2019, Framework Ventures made its name as an early backer of protocols in DeFi, or decentralized finance. Although Spencer and Anderson were alumni of Big Tech firms (Netflix and Snapchat, respectively), the duo quickly won over crypto’s anonymous “degens”—industry slang for degenerate traders. Their firm were early backers of the crypto lender Aave and the blockchain data network Chainlink, both of which have grown to become dominant protocols in DeFi.



Framework also grew, raising $100 million for a second fund in 2021 and then $400 million for a third the next year to invest in crypto. But now, the VC is touting its non-crypto bets, including the robotics data startup Mecka AI as well as a stake in the public mortgage issuer Better.com. The fund has already deployed about half of its fourth stash of capital, said Anderson. 



Inevitably, as AI saturates every nook and cranny of Silicon Valley (even the billboards), investors who got their start in crypto can’t help but keep up. In fact, Framework has hosted reading AI research paper reading sessions at its San Francisco office on the weekends, said Anderson. “Just because we’re crypto,” he said, “doesn’t mean that we haven’t been paying attention.”



See you next week,



Ben WeissX: @bdanweissEmail: benjamin.weiss@fortune.comSubmit a deal for the Term Sheet newsletter here.



Joey Abrams curated the deals section of today’s newsletter. Subscribe here.
